steps.title: Identify Reed's style
steps.content: The passage's main claim is that Reed uses broad forms rather than extreme accuracy. Both examples show stylized, not realistic, representations.

steps.title: Reject unsupported art claims
steps.content:
• Choice B: The passage mentions materials but not a pattern for choosing them.
  • Choice C: Reed depicts both a mythical character and a historical figure, so this focus is unsupported.
  • Choice D: The passage does not say his art became more abstract over time.

steps.title: State the central style claim
steps.content: The main idea is that Reed often represents subjects in a deliberately unrealistic or stylized way. The answer is A.
